ops.title: Advance TOCTOU investigation: working-copy sweep incident (2026-07-14)
ops.body:
## Incident
A colocated jj repo (user recap: repo-sync) swept uncommitted files off disk between commands mid-commit-sequence; the user recovered everything by hand from orphaned non-empty commits. User hypothesis: a reposync bug.

## Mechanism found (real, fixed in 845fb6cc)
jjRepo.Advance classified @ via a snapshot (probeWorkingCopy), then mutated several subprocesses later via `jj new <trunk>` / `jj rebase` — commands that take their OWN snapshot at execution time. Edits typed in between were swept into the outgoing commit (non-empty → survives as an anonymous visible head) while the tree reset to trunk. guard.stable only watched git HEAD + lock markers, never on-disk edits. Exposure: fsnotify watch includes .jj/repo/op_heads/heads (own jj activity summons a same-host sync ~15s later), the launchd reconcile tick bypasses the watch gate, and this host runs idle_threshold=5m.

## Attribution: plausible, not proven
Every reposync-issued `jj new` in the visible op logs of repo-sync AND synckit abandoned an *empty* predecessor — no direct forensic proof this incident was reposync's. No session transcript captured the sweep (only the user's one-sentence recap exists). Independent co-racer found and fixed the same day: ccstatusline ran `jj root` + `jj log …bookmarks` + `jj diff --stat` WITHOUT --ignore-working-copy every ~10s statusline refresh, snapshotting whatever repo the session sat in; both jj widgets were replaced with custom-command widgets running only --ignore-working-copy reads (verified zero ops across refreshes; backup at ~/.config/ccstatusline/settings.json.bak-20260714).

## Fix (commit 845fb6cc, cc-task b16944e9)
Post-mutation verification + forward-first recovery; OutcomeRecovered/OutcomeSwept; op-log shape ownership proof; PATH-shim race-injection tests. Details: cc-notes doc "Swept working-copy recovery in jj Advance mutations".

## Loose end closed
The previous session's recap claimed "both lessons are saved to memory" — no memory file was actually written that day (verified across all cc-pool accounts). This note is the durable record.
